region_of_origin: Central and Northern Europe (Germany, Scandinavia, Poland, Russia)
food_subgroup: Whole grain dark rye bread — 2 slices (~60g). Dense, low-GI bread made from whole rye flour or rye berries. Lowest GI of all commonly available bread types.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of dark rye bread is 2 slices weighing approximately 60g, typical of a 500g German-style whole grain rye loaf where slices are thinner and denser than white bread slices.

nutrient_source.title: Rye Bread, dark — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172682)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172682 is a direct match for dark whole-grain rye bread. All per-100g nutrient values are sourced from this entry. GI sourced separately from University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson et al., dark rye bread (whole grain)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI = 41 (low) for dark whole-grain rye bread per Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008), Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283. GL per serving = GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 41 × (48.3 × 0.60) / 100 = 41 × 28.98 / 100 ≈ 11.9; input record states GL ~9.8 using ~24g available carbohydrate per 60g serving (accounting for fibre subtraction from total carbohydrate), which is consistent with the source. GL ~9.8 retained as per input record consistent with University of Sydney methodology.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content — 470mg per 100g (282mg per 60g serving, approximately 14% of WHO 2g/day sodium limit)
hypertension.guidance: Dark rye bread contains moderate sodium (~282mg per 2-slice serving); patients with hypertension should account for this within their daily sodium budget, though the high arabinoxylan prebiotic fibre content supports gut microbiome diversity associated with SCFA production and indirect blood pressure regulation, and the magnesium content (~24mg per 60g) provides a minor vasodilatory benefit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 41) and low glycemic load (GL ~9.8 per 60g serving) — lowest GI of all commonly available bread types
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dark whole-grain rye bread is the preferred bread choice for patients with Type 2 Diabetes, with GI ~41 (low) and GL ~9.8 per 2-slice serving; the arabinoxylan (AXOS) fibre forms a highly viscous gel in the gut that significantly slows glucose absorption, making it substantially superior to white bread (GI ~71, GL ~23–25) and sourdough (GI ~55, GL ~20.6) at equivalent serving weights.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High dietary fibre (5.8g per 100g), very low saturated fat (0.3g per 100g), zero cholesterol, and zero trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Dark rye bread is cardioprotective due to its high whole-grain fibre content (5.8g dietary fibre per 100g), negligible saturated fat (0.3g/100g), zero cholesterol, and zero trans fat; regular whole-grain rye consumption is associated with reduced LDL cholesterol and improved cardiovascular risk markers per prospective cohort evidence, and the selenium content (28mcg/100g) supports antioxidant enzyme function relevant to vascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High arabinoxylan prebiotic fibre, selenium (28mcg/100g), and folate (58mcg/100g)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Dark rye bread supports anti-inflammatory gut microbiome health via arabinoxylan prebiotic fibre; its high selenium content (28mcg/100g) is required for glutathione peroxidase synthesis relevant to oxidative stress in RA, and its folate content (58mcg/100g, ~35mcg per 60g serving) is clinically relevant for patients on methotrexate therapy where folate supplementation is standard practice.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dark rye bread (whole grain, German/Scandinavian style) is a dense, moist, dark-coloured bread made from whole rye flour or coarsely ground rye berries, water, salt, and a sourdough starter or yeast. Pumpernickel, the darkest variety from the Westphalia region of Germany, is baked at very low temperature (~120°C) for 16–24 hours, producing its characteristic dark colour through a prolonged Maillard reaction without charring. Scandinavian varieties (rugbrød) are similarly dense and fibre-rich. The texture is compact and chewy with a slightly sour, earthy flavour. Dark rye bread has the lowest glycemic index of any commonly available bread type — GI ~41 (low), compared to white bread GI ~71 and sourdough GI ~55. Per 2 slices (~60g): approximately 149 kcal, 5.1g protein, 1.0g fat, 29g carbohydrate, 3.5g dietary fibre, GL ~9.8 (low), and ~282mg sodium. The low GI is mechanistically explained by arabinoxylan (AXOS) fibre, which forms an extremely viscous gel in the gastrointestinal tract that slows glucose absorption significantly. This makes dark rye bread the strongly preferred bread choice for patients with Type 2 Diabetes. Nutritionally, it is also rich in selenium (28mcg/100g), folate (58mcg/100g), magnesium (40mg/100g), iron (2.5mg/100g), and B vitamins. In Singapore, dark rye bread is available as Mestemacher brand (Cold Storage Finest, NTUC FairPrice Finest), Delba brand, and occasionally from artisan bakeries such as Starter Lab. It has been a dietary staple in Germany, Scandinavia, Poland, and Russia for over 1,000 years. Source: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172682) and University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008).
common_user_queries: is rye bread good for diabetics
whole grain rye bread glycemic index
dark bread for blood sugar control

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172682), an approved primary source with exact match to dark whole-grain rye bread. GI value of 41 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care), an approved GI-specific source with direct match. All core macros and secondary nutrients are complete and consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for dark rye bread not separately reported in USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172682 as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the primary source entry used. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA source). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based bread product with no animal-derived fat). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA source). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA source). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — no UV-exposed or fortified ingredients). GL per serving calculated as GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100; input record GL ~9.8 retained consistent with University of Sydney methodology using net available carbohydrate (~24g per 60g serving after fibre adjustment). This food is not a Singaporean dish; multilingual local names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are provided as Singapore-market consumer-facing translations to support retrieval in the Singapore context, not as native-cuisine mandatory fields. Ontology codes (FoodOn FOODON_03307148, FoodEx2 A00HL, SNOMED CT 229046006) carried forward from input record and assessed as defensible for rye bread.
